wiki.summary: IRIS OUT is a single by Japanese artist Kenshi Yonezu (米津玄師), released on September 15, 2025, serving as the opening theme song for the animated film Chainsaw Man – The Movie: Reze Arc. The 2:32-minute track, produced entirely by Yonezu himself (who handled lyrics, composition, arrangement, vocals, production, programming, and arranging), became one of the most impactful Japanese pop releases of the last few years, shattering Japan's daily streaming records and becoming the fastest song in Japan to reach 100 million streams. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: IRIS OUT is a single by Japanese artist Kenshi Yonezu (米津玄師), released on September 15, 2025, serving as the opening theme song for the animated film Chainsaw Man – The Movie: Reze Arc. The 2:32-minute track, produced entirely by Yonezu himself (who handled lyrics, composition, arrangement, vocals, production, programming, and arranging), became one of the most impactful Japanese pop releases of the last few years, shattering Japan's daily streaming records and becoming the fastest song in Japan to reach 100 million streams. It peaked at No. 1 on the Billboard Japan Hot 100 and reached No. 5 on the Billboard Global 200, achieving the highest-ever charting position for a Japanese-language song on that chart. The song's musical style features frantic keys and sudden rhythm shifts that mimic emotional destabilization, with lyrics describing a narrator spiraling under emotional hunger where desire, fear, pleasure, and self-destruction intertwine. The physical single was released as IRIS OUT / JANE DOE (Yonezu's 16th single) on October 24, 2025, including an ending theme "JANE DOE" performed with Hikaru Utada. Yonezu created new illustrated cover art featuring the character Reze for the release. Read more on Last.fm.
